game_name: Street Fighter II Turbo: Hyper Fighting

element_introduction_order.first_appears: early matches vs CPU / arcade progression
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: new special moves added in Turbo for most characters; requires precise timing due to increased game speed
new mobility option requiring aerial timing mastery
directional attack variation expanding zoning options
new ranged tool for character previously without projectile
aerial mobility and anti-air capability combined
spatial repositioning mechanic allowing mid-range instant transport; high skill ceiling
faster game speed requires tighter timing precision for all special moves and combos; game speed adjustable 1-6 settings

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: High intensity, fast-paced arcade fighting with turbo speed increasing reaction demands; round-based tension cycles with risk-reward special move execution windows

difficulty_curve.early_game: Accessible matches against weaker CPU opponents; turbo speed adjustable down to normal Champion Edition pace via menu setting
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Increased CPU difficulty and aggression; opponents begin exploiting new special moves; demands precision timing
difficulty_curve.late_game: Grand Masters (final opponents) present significant difficulty spike; faster frame-data demands require mastery of character-specific new moves and turbo speed mechanics
notable_spikes: Transition to Turbo speed from normal mode significantly increases input precision requirements
Grand Master CPU difficulty spike at arcade progression end
Character rebalancing in Turbo creates matchup-specific challenges not present in Champion Edition

notable_level_patterns.description: Sequential CPU matches culminating in Grand Master fight; no branching paths. Matches increase in difficulty. Player faces 8 regular opponents then 4 Grand Masters
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Standard arcade progression ending with M. Bison final boss

notable_level_patterns.description: Restored in SNES Turbo port after removal in original SNES SF2; provides tension relief and combo practice between matches
SNES version allows switching between Turbo rules and Champion Edition Normal mode; speed adjustable 1-4 default (1-6 with cheat code)
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Menu-selectable mode affecting all subsequent matches
